spec_match.rationale: The page exercises iframe focus attempts and generates the correct policy strings, but the local demo route does not send distinct Permissions-Policy response headers for each model, so the deny/self/all comparison is partly simulated.

interactive.rationale: The policy selector, load-time replay, user-activated replay, top-level input, and embedded-frame control all provide real interactive state and visible decision logs.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page directly explores the feature's purpose: preventing embedded content from moving focus without user activation while preserving explicitly allowed behavior.

edge_cases.rationale: Deny, same-origin, and allow-all models are represented alongside load-time and user-activated focus paths and an unsupported-browser warning.

references.rationale: The feature page and concept link to ChromeStatus feature 5179186249465856 and the focus-without-user-activation specification.

conformance.rationale: The suite checks six distinct contracts; 5/6 pass in Headless Chrome 149 and the only failure is the v151 feature name not yet appearing in this older browser's permissions-policy feature list.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP exercised all three policy selections and both replay buttons, observed iframe readiness/focus logs and generated policy changes, and found no runtime errors after the quote-escaping repair.

telemetry_health.rationale: The page and iframe remained responsive throughout the interactions with no unexpected telemetry, resource, or assertion failures.

design_system.rationale: The demo uses the shared visual system, native controls, readable text logs, and visible state rather than a static code card.

accessibility.rationale: DevTools exposes named native controls, a titled iframe, labelled textboxes, keyboard-operable buttons/select, and textual equivalents for focus state and policy output.

openQuestions.title: Back the policy matrix with real response headers
openQuestions.detail: Add server-backed routes that send deny, self, and allow-all Permissions-Policy headers so each iframe focus result proves the network policy contract rather than relying mainly on generated snippets and the iframe allow attribute.

summary: The exhaustive sweep found a parse-blocking quote-escaping bug in the generated deny-policy iframe snippet. Commit 71d6dc28 replaced the invalid single-quoted string with a template literal. DevTools MCP now loads the page, exercises the selector and both replay paths, exposes complete control semantics, and reports only the expected older-browser origin-trial warning.
